entries.description: Set 029 initialized: orchestrator model + effort indicator gauges (4 sessions). Operator-initiated feature request. NTE budget pending operator confirmation at set creation; estimated $0.55-$1.55 routed spend across the set (revised at S1 resume to $1.15-$1.75 actual+forecast).

Session 1 step 1: design audit proposal authored at docs/proposals/2026-05-17-model-effort-gauges-design-audit/proposal.md (~400 LOC, self-contained, 6 open questions, JSON response format requested).

Session 1 step 2 WAIVED per memory feedback_ai_router_usage (router reserved for end-of-session verification). The originally-spec'd route_audit.py was not authored; the audit was conducted by manual paste-and-collect against GPT-5.4 + Gemini Pro at $0.00. Waiver re-confirmed by operator at 2026-05-18 session resume; recorded durably in spec.md Session 1 step 2.

Session 1 step 3: cross-provider audit verdicts received via manual paste-and-collect. GPT-5.4 returned a comprehensive prose review with primary-doc citations (covers all 6 open questions + 3 escalations + 4 showstoppers). Gemini Pro returned freeform commentary covering Q5 + 2 escalations (silent on Q1/Q2/Q3/Q4/Q6). Raw responses preserved at docs/proposals/2026-05-17-model-effort-gauges-design-audit/{gpt-5-4,gemini-pro}-result.json. Cost: $0.00.

Session 1 step 4: audit-summary.md authored synthesizing both verdicts. Q1-Q6 locked. Five showstoppers resolved with mitigations (S1: Stop hook lacks model field -> use SessionStart; S2: /think* as live effort -> Medium default + last-observed treatment; S3: initialSize not a real VS Code property -> drop, use Playwright screenshot tests; S4: Stop timing lagging -> same fix as S1; S5: Windows atomic-write contention -> retry loop). Marker schema bumped to v2 with signalKind + confidence fields. Manual-override quickpick spec'd with MRU + hotkey-bindable args.

Session 1 step 5: spec.md Q1-Q6 marked RESOLVED with one-liner pointers to audit-summary.md sections. D8 (hook installer) rewritten to reflect Claude-only auto-detection in v1. Effort normalization table revised (Claude column: Medium default + last-observed for /think*). Sessions 2 and 3 steps rewritten (SessionStart hook not Stop; retry loop; visual-treatment matrix for 4 signalKind values; drop initialSize). Risks R5 and R6 added.

Session 1 step 6 (Round A): session-verification call against the audit synthesis. Verifier: gpt-5-4. Verdict: REJECTED with 12 must-fix items (5 Bucket-1 doc-accuracy drift + 7 Bucket-2 design refinements). Cost: $0.264.

Session 1 step 7 (NEW class — in-session consensus): the 7 Bucket-2 design refinements routed through cross-engine consensus call (gpt-5-4 + gemini-pro) per the new memory feedback_prefer_ai_consensus_over_human_prompt (saved this session from operator's direct directive). Both engines accepted the proposed direction on all seven items; gpt-5-4 added five tightening modifications (race-window re-read, attempt-count math, scrollable-not-horizontally wording, confidence-low producer rule, dual-condition /clear check) which were absorbed as strictly-improving refinements. Cost: $0.085 total ($0.080 gpt-5-4 + $0.004 gemini-pro).

Session 1 step 8: applied all 12 Round-A must-fix items + GPT's 5 consensus tightenings to audit-summary.md and spec.md. Major additions: multi-writer precedence policy (new section in audit-summary, updates to spec R4/Session 2 step 5/Session 3 step 1); revised visual-treatment matrix (stripes are stale-only, configured-default = dashed rim + DEFAULT pill, last-observed = hollow rim + clock-icon overlay + time-elapsed); retry ceiling bumped to 5 attempts/2050ms; confidence-low producer rule locked; /clear dual-condition verification gating; R7 added; container-height limitation documented; routing-notes section rewritten to reflect the manual-paste audit + new in-session consensus call class.

Session 1 step 9 (Round B): confirmation pass. All 12 Round-A must-fix items marked ADDRESSED; one new drift item surfaced — spec.md Goal-state section (lines 85-92, not in Round A's bundle) still contained pre-audit wording (>1h stale threshold, install CTA on stale, Claude=Stop hook). Fix applied verbatim from D8/Q6 locked decisions: 8h staleness, no-CTA-on-stale, SessionStart hook, Codex auto-watcher, Gemini/Copilot manual-only. Cost: $0.138.

Session 1 step 10 (Round C): Goal-state fix marked ADDRESSED; surfaced two more pre-audit drift items in previously-uninspected sections of spec.md ('per-surface hooks' wording understated D8's hook/shim/manual-writer mix; Session 3 'Creates' list still included installOrchestratorHookCodex.ts + '4 new commands' contradicting D8's 'Codex auto-watcher, no user-facing install'). Both fixed mechanically from D8 locked text. Per memory feedback_verifier_spiral_recruit_codex, did NOT chain a Round D — pattern was each round exposing different un-bundled regions of spec.md, not new design issues against the same content. Cost: $0.358 (higher than typical p50=$0.13 due to gpt-5-4 emitting 22k output tokens; flag for future bundle sizing).

Session 2 boundary write: ai_router.start_session flipped session-state.json to currentSession=2, status=in-progress, lifecycleState=work_in_progress, orchestrator=claude-code/anthropic/claude-opus-4-7/high. work_started event appended to session-events.jsonl. ai-assignment.md S1 actuals recorded ($0.845 routed vs. $0.10-$0.30 original estimate; overshoot is process-related — three verification rounds plus one Bucket-2 consensus call vs. the planned single-call shape; reflects two newly-saved process memories that changed verification shape mid-S1).

Pre-implementation verification per spec.md Session 2 steps 5 + 6 (factual question: does Claude /clear fire SessionStart, does /clear represent a fresh-session boundary semantically, does UserPromptSubmit expose message text). WebFetch against the official Claude Code hooks docs at https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks confirmed all three preconditions: (1) /clear fires SessionStart with source: "clear" (matcher table in the docs lists startup / resume / clear / compact as the four source values); (2) /think* are per-message thinking-budget escalations, not persistent session settings, so /clear is by definition a fresh-session boundary — R7 dual condition is TRUE; (3) UserPromptSubmit payload includes a `prompt` field with the full user-submitted text, so /think* / /megathink / /ultrathink prefix detection ships at full functionality without the Medium-only fallback. Design decision: SessionStart hook clobbers effort to Medium on ALL source values (startup/resume/clear/compact) — simpler than per-source branching and lagging-signal-resistant per the audit's design intent. Cost: $0.00 (WebFetch is unbilled).

package.json updates: (a) registered dabblerOrchestratorIndicator as type:webview in views.dabblerSessionSetsContainer, ordered FIRST (above dabblerSessionSets). No initialSize per audit S3 — container height cannot be guaranteed. (b) Declared three new commands: dabbler.installOrchestratorHook.claudeCode, dabbler.setOrchestrator, dabbler.openOrchestratorWriterLog. (c) Dropped explicit activationEvents array (was [workspaceContains:docs/session-sets]) since modern VS Code engine auto-generates these from contributes — IDE diagnostic flagged the entry as redundant. View contribution + onView semantics handle activation automatically.

scripts/write-orchestrator-marker.js implemented (~390 LOC). Four invocation modes: --mode session-start | user-prompt-submit | manual | configured-default. Multi-writer precedence policy: reads existing marker, compares signalKind precedence (current > manual > last-observed > configured-default), re-reads immediately before atomic rename to close the TOCTOU race window, skips write if proposed signal is weaker than fresh existing signal, appends one JSON-lines entry to ~/.dabbler/orchestrator-writer.log on skips. Stale signals (age > stalenessMaxSec, default 28800s = 8h) never block fresh writes per audit policy. Confidence-low producer rule: emits confidence:low + model:unknown + modelDisplayName:"Claude (model unknown)" when SessionStart payload's .model is missing/null/unparseable. Atomic write via tmp + rename. Retry loop: 5 attempts (initial + 4) at 50/200/600/1200ms backoff (~2050ms total ceiling) for Windows file-watcher contention per audit S5 REVISED. user-prompt-submit mode: detects /think (high), /megathink (extra-high), /ultrathink (max) prefixes in payload.prompt; preserves top-level model signal and updates only effort.*; bootstraps a Medium-default Claude marker if none exists; exits cleanly (no write) on prompts without a /think* prefix. Smoke-tested directly via bash: six paths (current/Opus, /think effort merge, missing-model confidence-low, no-/think no-write, Codex skipped, manual force-override) all behave correctly; one log entry written for the configured-default skip.

src/providers/orchestratorIndicatorProvider.ts (~380 LOC) + media/orchestrator-indicator/indicator.css (~190 LOC) implemented. Provider is a vscode.WebviewViewProvider for viewType dabblerOrchestratorIndicator. Watcher: vscode.workspace.createFileSystemWatcher(RelativePattern(MARKER_DIR, current-orchestrator.json)) with 60s poll backstop and 50ms render debounce for Windows atomic-write coalescing. Renders two side-by-side SVG semi-circle gauges + thinking LED (hidden on providers without a native concept). Visual-treatment matrix per audit REVISED 2026-05-18: current=solid+solid rim, configured-default=85% opacity + dashed rim + DEFAULT pill, last-observed=hollow rim + filled needle + clock-icon overlay + (last /think Xm ago) suffix, manual=solid + operator-icon overlay. Stale state is signal-agnostic diagonal-stripe overlay at 50% opacity (NOT used for configured-default — stripes are stale-only). Tooltip copy embeds confidence explicitly per audit visual matrix. Last-updated annotation always visible. Empty state: solid grey gauges + "No signal — install hook" CTA that dispatches dabbler.installOrchestratorHook.claudeCode. HTML uses CSP nonce; webview message-passing for the install CTA + future setOrchestrator + writer-log shortcuts.

src/commands/installOrchestratorHookClaudeCode.ts (~170 LOC). Idempotent installer: locates existing dabbler entries by matcher + write-orchestrator-marker.js substring in command path and upgrades them in place; appends new entries if absent; preserves foreign hooks verbatim. Atomic write to ~/.claude/settings.json (tmp + rename). Per Set 029 audit Q5 locked design + the pre-implementation verification finding, installs SessionStart hooks with all four source matchers (startup/resume/clear/compact) plus one UserPromptSubmit hook without a matcher (fires on every prompt; the helper short-circuits non-/think* prompts at zero cost). Shows an information message on success with a one-click jump to settings.json. Stub commands also shipped: src/commands/setOrchestratorManualStub.ts (surfaces a 'coming in 0.14.3' dialog with one-click jump to the Claude installer until Session 3 lands the full quickpick), src/commands/openOrchestratorWriterLog.ts (opens ~/.dabbler/orchestrator-writer.log for diagnostics).

src/extension.ts wired up: registered OrchestratorIndicatorProvider against viewType dabblerOrchestratorIndicator; registered three sibling commands via safeRegister blocks. esbuild + tsc --noEmit + tsc --outDir out all clean (EXIT=0). Unit suite (npm run test:unit): 376 passing, 2 failing; both failures (notificationsSection — rendering, configEditor-foundation — panel lifecycle) reproduce on a clean master without my changes (verified via git stash + re-run), so they're pre-existing flakes from Set 026/Set 030-era tests, not regressions from this session.

src/test/playwright/orchestrator-indicator.spec.ts authored with 8 scenarios: (A) current Opus marker → tier-flagship + signal-current classes + label; (B) Haiku marker → tier-low classes + label; (C) confidence-low marker → tooltip text 'low confidence' + 'hook payload missing model'; (D) effort.signalKind=last-observed → clock-overlay + 'last /think 12m ago' suffix; (E) configured-default → DEFAULT pill + NO stripes (stripes are stale-only per REVISED 2026-05-18 audit decision); (F) 9h-old marker → .gauges.stale class + 'last updated 9h ago — stale' annotation; (G) absent marker → empty-state CTA + 'install hook' link; (H) helper-script multi-writer precedence (non-Electron, runs the script directly via child_process). Scenario H ran and passed in 1.2s (npm run test:playwright -- --grep 'helper script skips'). Full Electron suite (7 launch-based scenarios) running in background — Playwright config has workers=1 because each scenario launches a real VS Code Electron instance and they can't share user-data-dirs.

Layer 3 Playwright initial run: 7 of 7 Electron orchestrator scenarios timed out on the iframe selector iframe[title*="Orchestrator"] (the spec assumption that VS Code titles its webview iframes by view name — which it does NOT). Two pre-existing treeView.spec.ts failures unrelated (Refresh-via-command-palette can't find quick-input widget; reproduces on clean master). Diagnostic spec confirmed: VS Code wraps webview views in iframe.webview with src=vscode-webview://... and NO title. The actual content iframe (vscode-webview://.../fake.html?id=...) is added PROGRAMMATICALLY by the outer iframe's service-worker bootstrap, so it has no <iframe> element in the outer DOM and Playwright's frameLocator chains can't see it. Fix: replaced openIndicatorFrame to use the lower-level Frame API — page.locator('iframe.webview').elementHandle() -> contentFrame() -> childFrames() — with a retry loop that probes each child frame for the .container element. Returns a Frame (not FrameLocator) but the .locator() API is shape-identical so the assertions didn't need changes. All 8 scenarios pass in 1.5 minutes after the fix.

Operator on-device feedback after seeing the rendered gauges: (1) effort gauge Medium rendered with too-short color arc (~1/3 fill at -120° vs. Model gauge flagship at -30° ~5/6 fill — jarring visual imbalance); (2) gauges + fonts need to be bigger; (3) gauges need to be responsive (wrap second gauge when panel narrow). Applied in-session: Medium effort needle re-centered from -120° to -90° (gauge midpoint, half-fill) with other levels redistributed (Low -150, High -60, Extra-High -35, Max -15). Gauge SVG dimensions 70x38 -> 100x54 (~43% bigger). Font sizes bumped ~40-50%: gauge-cell 10->14px, gauge-suffix 9->12px, last-updated 9->12px, empty-state 11->14px, default-pill 8->10px, overlays 11->14px, thinking LED 7->10px. Container max-height 100->150px. Responsive wrap: grid-template-columns flips from 1fr 1fr to 1fr at panel width <260px. The audit-locked '<=100px' D3 phrasing is now stale; updated provider header comment and CSS top-of-file comment to note the 2026-05-18 revision. Memory project_gauges_sizing_followup.md updated to reflect that this is now SHIPPED, not pending. CHANGELOG gains a 'Mid-S2 sizing + responsive-wrap revision' section. Re-ran Playwright suite — all 8 scenarios still green (assertions check CSS classes, not pixel dimensions).

package.json version 0.14.1 → 0.14.2. Spec drift correction: spec.md Session 2 step 8 directed a 0.13.17 → 0.13.18 bump authored on 2026-05-17 before Set 030 shipped its 0.14.x line; corrected to patch-bump from the current 0.14.1. CHANGELOG.md gets a new [0.14.2] section under [Unreleased] documenting: webview view + visual-treatment matrix per signalKind; tooltip copy with confidence embedded; install command (idempotent SessionStart×4 source matchers + UserPromptSubmit); manual-override stub; writer-log opener; write-orchestrator-marker.js helper with all four modes + multi-writer precedence + retry loop + confidence-low producer; effort-reset on every SessionStart (R7 verified TRUE both conditions). Known limitations: starting-model-only (no runtime /model detection in v1), container height not guaranteed, non-Claude surfaces ship in 0.14.3, manual quickpick is a stub until 0.14.3. CLAUDE.md 'Extension versioning' section updated to v0.14.2 with the Claude-only v1 preview description and the 0.14.3 forward-pointer.

Round A verification (marker writer + CSS visual matrix, ~605 LOC bundle, split per memory feedback_split_large_verification_bundles to stay under the 700 LOC ceiling). Verifier: gpt-5-4. Verdict: not-ready, 3 must-fix items: (1) TOCTOU race in attemptWriteWithPrecedence — read-once-decide-write pattern leaves a race window; (2) UserPromptSubmit merge/bootstrap can clobber fresher top-level SessionStart markers because the merge atomicWrite bypasses precedence and writes a stale snapshot; (3) stale stripes were implemented as background-image on .gauge-svg which paints BEHIND the SVG content, not as a true overlay — also alpha was 18% vs. the audit's 50% target. Plus a should-fix: audit-summary.md and spec.md still say ≤100px despite the mid-S2 revision to 150px. All four addressed: (1) re-read-immediately-before-rename added in attemptWriteWithPrecedence with skip-on-stronger-fresh-marker + new log reason 'weaker-than-existing-on-reread'; (2) merge branch reworked to read latest snapshot just before tmp-write+rename (initial superseded the bootstrap); (3) stripes moved to .stale .gauge-cell::before pseudo-element at z-index 2 with 45% alpha; (4) superseding note added to both audit-summary.md and spec.md D3. Cost: $0.152.

Round B verification (provider + installer, ~565 LOC bundle). Verifier: gpt-5-4. Verdict: not-ready, 2 must-fix items: (1) renderLoaded effort suffix branches (default/manual) keyed off marker.signalKind (top-level model signal) instead of marker.effort.signalKind — a Codex configured-default session with /think* observation would render '(default)' on the effort gauge instead of the elapsed-time suffix; (2) gauge angle math in renderGaugeSvg used a `180 + needleAngleDeg` offset that inverted the y-axis, sending -90° DOWN instead of UP — all needle/fill endpoints were BELOW the visible viewBox. Plus a non-blocking follow-up: consider a parent-directory watcher so first creation of ~/.dabbler/current-orchestrator.json is picked up immediately. Both must-fix items addressed: (1) effort suffix branches now check marker.effort.signalKind; (2) removed the 180+ offset, use angle directly — SVG's y-down axis naturally inverts sin direction so cy + radius*sin(-90°) = cy - radius (top center) is correct. Also simplified largeArc to always 0 since upper-semicircle arcs are always ≤180°. Cost: $0.206.

Session 3 step 1: rewrote scripts/write-orchestrator-marker.js (~613 LOC) for marker schema v3 + per-set identity. Added walkUpResolveSet(startCwd) that walks from cwd upward looking for docs/session-sets/, then scans for the single in-progress set; returns slug + setDir on success or a reason (no-docs-session-sets, no-in-progress-set, multiple-in-progress-sets) on failure. New ensurePerSetMarkerDir(perSetMarkerDir) drops a self-protecting .gitignore (containing '*' then '!.gitignore') alongside the marker on first create — consumer repos inherit untracked-marker behavior without needing a workspace-root patch. buildMarker() adds sessionSetSlug + bumps SCHEMA_VERSION to 3; mergeEffort() also re-stamps both so a marker that survives a cross-set boundary converges on the right slug. Fail-closed posture: on resolver failure the writer logs reason+candidates+cwd to ~/.dabbler/orchestrator-writer.log and exits 0 (semantically a no-op). New --cwd CLI flag pins the resolution root for tests.

Session 3 step 2: rewrote orchestratorIndicatorProvider.ts for per-set path resolution. Added module-level resolveActiveSet() running the same walk-up algorithm rooted at vscode.workspace.workspaceFolders[0]. The provider now uses TWO watchers: a state watcher on docs/session-sets/*/session-state.json that fires on close-out flips / start_session events and triggers rebindMarkerWatcher(), and a per-set marker watcher rebound whenever resolution changes. computeState() resolves first, then reads + parses the marker, then runs the slug-integrity check; mismatch returns kind:'empty' (falls through to the empty-state CTA).

Session 3 step 3: extracted SessionSetsModel.ts (~131 LOC) from SessionSetsProvider.ts. Pulled progressText, isCurrentSessionInFlight, iconUriFor, needsMigrationBadge, forceClosedBadge, modeBadge, touchedDate, uatBadge, bucketSets, sortBucket, ICON_FILES out of the provider. SessionSetsProvider.ts collapsed to a thin VS Code adapter (~246 LOC) that delegates bucket/sort decisions to the model. Re-exports forceClosedBadge, isCurrentSessionInFlight, modeBadge, needsMigrationBadge, progressText so cancelTreeView.test.ts and forceClosedBadge.test.ts (which import from this module) don't break. Layer-2 unit test sessionSetsProvider.test.ts repointed to import directly from SessionSetsModel (canonical home). Both the current native tree and the future S4 custom webview tree now consume the same data layer.

Session 3 step 4: rewrote src/test/playwright/orchestrator-indicator.spec.ts (~751 LOC, 12 scenarios A-L). Existing A-H scenarios updated to seed markers at the per-set path (seed.set_dir/.dabbler/orchestrator.json) and call startSession(seed, 1) so the resolver finds an in-progress set; all markers now declare schemaVersion: 3 + sessionSetSlug: seed.slug. New scenarios: I (mismatched sessionSetSlug -> empty state), J (helper-script ambiguous resolution -> write skipped, log carries reason: multiple-in-progress-sets + candidates), K (helper happy path verifies schema-v3 marker + slug match + .gitignore self-protect presence + content), L (helper cwd outside any docs/session-sets/ -> skip, log carries reason: no-docs-session-sets, no legacy global marker).

Session 3 step 5: package.json version 0.14.2 -> 0.15.0 (minor bump per audit consensus Q9). CHANGELOG [0.15.0] section authored documenting the breaking-within-the-v0.14.2-preview changes (marker schema v3, per-set identity, walk-up resolver, fail-closed posture, watcher re-binding, .gitignore self-protect, SessionSetsModel extraction, R8 wrong-set-attachment risk, R9 gitignore-not-auto-patched risk). New docs/orchestrator-marker-schema.md (208 LOC) — authoritative reference for v3 marker shape, walk-up resolver, fail-closed posture, multi-writer precedence. .gitignore patched at workspace root with `docs/session-sets/*/.dabbler/` as belt-and-suspenders.

Session 3 step 6: TypeScript compiles clean (npx tsc, no errors). npm run test:unit: 376 passing, 2 failing — both pre-existing (configEditor-foundation stub harness ViewColumn.One undefined; notificationsSection 'wired in Set 026 Session 7' label assertion). pytest 585 passed, 1 skipped — three pre-existing ImportErrors on migrate_router_config tests due to missing ruamel module (unrelated). Smoke-tested the writer against this very repo: helper wrote a valid schema-v3 marker at docs/session-sets/029-orchestrator-model-effort-gauges/.dabbler/orchestrator.json with the self-protect .gitignore; git status confirmed the directory is fully ignored.

Session 3 step 7: Round A verification (writer + schema doc, ~38k chars). The initial single-round bundle at 101k chars hit gpt-5-4 429 on the OpenAI Responses endpoint; the retry of just Round A at 37k chars ALSO hit 429 — the sticky-rate-limit window outlasted the failed call. Switched verifier to model='gemini-pro' (different provider; satisfies cross-provider verification since the orchestrator is Claude). Verifier: gemini-pro. Verdict: VERIFIED clean across Q1 (walk-up algorithm), Q2 (sessionSetSlug + schema v3), Q3 (fail-closed posture), Q4 (.gitignore self-protect). Cost: $0.018.

Session 3 step 8: Round B verification (reader + SessionSetsModel + refactored SessionSetsProvider + Playwright spec + CHANGELOG, ~95k chars). Verifier: gemini-pro. Verdict: MUST-FIX (3): Q5 slug-validation truthiness bug — 'marker.sessionSetSlug && ...' would let null/empty-string through as 'absent' rather than 'mismatch'; Q6 setUpStateWatcher() only called at view resolution — if workspaceFolders is empty then, the watcher never binds and the 60s poll is the only signal; Q8 spec says reader 'logs' on slug mismatch but implementation fell silent. Plus one SUGGEST (deferred): end-to-end ambiguous scenario launching VS Code with two in-progress sets. Cost: $0.047.

Session 3 step 9: applied all three Round-B MUST-FIX items to orchestratorIndicatorProvider.ts. Q5: tightened slug check from 'if (marker.sessionSetSlug && ...)' to 'if (marker.sessionSetSlug !== undefined && ...)' — null/empty-string now correctly fail-closed to empty state; only an actually-omitted field (forward-compat for hypothetical v4) passes. Q6: added private workspaceFoldersListener field + vscode.workspace.onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders subscription in resolveWebviewView; on fire, disposes the stale state watcher and re-runs setUpStateWatcher() + rebindMarkerWatcher() + scheduleRender(). Listener disposed by tearDownWatchers(). Q8: added lazy getOutputChannel() creating 'Dabbler Orchestrator Indicator' on first append; slug-mismatch branch in computeState() now logs timestamped line with both slugs + the resolved marker path before returning kind:'empty'. SUGGEST deferred to S4. TypeScript re-compiled clean.

Session 3 step 10: Round C confirmation pass — bundled ONLY the post-fix reader source (~46k chars) and asked the verifier to confirm F1 (slug-presence), F2 (workspace-folders listener), F3 (mismatch log). Verifier: gemini-pro. Verdict: VERIFIED across all three Fs. 'The slug validation is now stricter, the state watcher correctly binds when workspace folders change after activation, and slug mismatches are properly logged for diagnostics.' Cost: $0.020. Total S3 routed cost: $0.085 (A $0.018 + B $0.047 + C $0.020). Round-by-round finding count: 0 -> 3 -> 0 (clean convergence, no spiral per feedback_verifier_spiral_recruit_codex).

Round C confirmation pass: re-bundled the full post-fix marker writer + provider + CSS and asked the verifier to confirm each of the 5 must-fix items was actually fixed and no new bugs introduced. Verifier: gpt-5-4. Verdict: 4 of 5 fully resolved, 1 partially resolved — the user-prompt-submit branch's re-read happened BEFORE the tmp file write rather than immediately before rename, leaving a residual race window. Verifier also confirmed: (a) no new must-fix items introduced; (b) the largeArc=0 simplification renders correctly at needleAngleDeg=0 (180° diameter chord); (c) the bootstrap-discard-when-late-read-finds-marker pattern is correct including the parse-fail-corrupt edge case. Applied tightening: restructured user-prompt-submit branch so readExistingMarker happens inside the try block immediately before writeFileSync+renameSync. The residual window is now equivalent to attemptWriteWithPrecedence's residual window — minimal and acceptable. Smoke-tested both merge and bootstrap paths after the tightening. Per memory feedback_verifier_spiral_recruit_codex: the round-by-round finding count (3->2->1) is convergence, not spiral. No Round D needed. Cost: $0.220. Total S2 verification cost: $0.578 (A $0.152 + B $0.206 + C $0.220).
